# ctxerror

```go
import "github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
```

Package ctxerror provides errors that can carry structured metadata \(context and tags\). The data model matches Sentry's contexts/tags and can be uploaded to Sentry.

Use context for richer, grouped data \(e.g. request info, payload fragments\) and tags for small string key/value attributes \(e.g. operation, region\).

By default, metadata is shared across an error chain to minimize allocations. For errors that are reused \(e.g. global errors\), call \(\*Err\).Unshare\(\) to ensure they maintain a private copy of their metadata.

## Variables

<a name="ContextDefaultMapSize"></a>ContextDefaultMapSize is the default size for context maps. It is used to avoid unnecessary allocations when adding context to an error. You can change this value if you expect to have more or less context groups and tags.

```go
var ContextDefaultMapSize = 12
```

<a name="Context"></a>
## type [Context](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L29>)

Context holds metadata attached to an error chain. Use Context for richer, grouped data \(for example request information or payload fragments\). Context is intended as easy extra data for structured logging and error catchers \(e.g. Sentry\).

```go
type Context = map[string]map[string]any
```

<a name="GetContext"></a>
### func [GetContext](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L354>)

```go
func GetContext(err error) Context
```

GetContext returns the context of the given error chain. If there are multiple context maps in the chain, they will be merged together. If there are duplicate keys, the value of the outermost error will be used. If no context was set, an empty context map is returned. Do not modify the returned context directly.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
)

func main() {
	base := ctxerror.New("db error").
		SetContext("req", map[string]any{"id": "r1"})
	wrapped := ctxerror.Wrap(base, "service error").
		SetContext("user", map[string]any{"id": "u1"})

	ctx := ctxerror.GetContext(wrapped)
	fmt.Println(ctx["req"]["id"], ctx["user"]["id"])

}
```

#### Output

```
r1 u1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err"></a>
## type [Err](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L67-L77>)

Err is an error type that allows attaching structured metadata, known as context and tags. This is useful for adding supplementary information to errors, which can then be retrieved for logging or sent to error tracking services like Sentry.

The attached context and tags are not included in the error message returned by the Error\(\) method. They can be accessed using the [GetContext](<#GetContext>) and [GetTags](<#GetTags>) functions.

By default, Err uses a shared metadata model. This means that metadata is shared across a single error chain to optimize performance by reducing allocations. When you add context or tags to an error, that metadata becomes part of a common pool for the entire chain.

If you intend to reuse an error \(for example, a global error variable\) across different error chains, you should call Unshare\(\) on it. This creates a private copy of the metadata, preventing modifications in one chain from affecting another.

```go
type Err struct {
    bruh.Err
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Err.SetContext"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [SetContext](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L150>)

```go
func (e *Err) SetContext(key string, value map[string]any) ModifiableContextErr
```

SetContext adds the given key\-value pairs to the context of the error.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
)

func main() {
	err := ctxerror.New("request failed").
		SetContext("req", map[string]any{"id": "r1"}).
		SetContext("user", map[string]any{"id": "u1"})
	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetContext(err)["req"]["id"], ctxerror.GetContext(err)["user"]["id"])

}
```

#### Output

```
r1 u1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.SetContexts"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [SetContexts](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L170>)

```go
func (e *Err) SetContexts(context Context) ModifiableContextErr
```

SetContexts adds all key\-value pairs from \`context\` to the error context.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
)

func main() {
	err := ctxerror.New("request failed").SetContexts(ctxerror.Context{
		"req":  {"id": "r1"},
		"user": {"id": "u1"},
	})
	context := ctxerror.GetContext(err)
	fmt.Println(context["req"]["id"], context["user"]["id"])

}
```

#### Output

```
r1 u1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.SetTag"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [SetTag](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L190>)

```go
func (e *Err) SetTag(key, value string) ModifiableContextErr
```

SetTag adds a single key\-value pair to the error tags.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
)

func main() {
	err := ctxerror.New("request failed").
		SetTag("env", "prod").
		SetTag("region", "eu")
	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetTags(err)["env"], ctxerror.GetTags(err)["region"])

}
```

#### Output

```
prod eu
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.SetTags"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [SetTags](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L204>)

```go
func (e *Err) SetTags(tags Tags) ModifiableContextErr
```

SetTags adds all key\-value pairs from tags to the error tags.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
)

func main() {
	err := ctxerror.New("request failed").
		SetTags(ctxerror.Tags{"env": "prod", "region": "eu"})
	tags := ctxerror.GetTags(err)
	fmt.Println(tags["env"], tags["region"])

	// or with pre-allocated map, so later added tags don't cause map allocations
	tagsMap := make(ctxerror.Tags, 12)
	tagsMap["env"] = "prod"
	err = ctxerror.New("request failed").
		SetTags(tagsMap)
	err = ctxerror.Wrap(err, "another error").
		SetTag("region", "eu")
	tags = ctxerror.GetTags(err)
	fmt.Println(tags["env"], tags["region"])

}
```

#### Output

```
prod eu
prod eu
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.Unshare"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Unshare](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L221>)

```go
func (e *Err) Unshare() ModifiableContextErr
```

Unshare makes the error keep its own private copies of context and tags. Call this when you plan to make an error global or reuse it across independent wrappers. It deep\-copies the maps and marks the error as unshared \(shared=false\).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
)

func main() {
	base := ctxerror.New("db").
		SetContext("req", map[string]any{"path": "/v1"}).
		Unshare()

	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetContext(base)["req"]["path"]) // -> /v1

	// because of the call to `Unshare` wrapped has its own private copy of context
	wrapped := ctxerror.Wrap(base, "left").SetContext("req", map[string]any{"path": "/v2"})

	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetContext(wrapped)["req"]["path"]) // -> /v2
	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetContext(base)["req"]["path"])    // -> /v1

}
```

#### Output

```
/v1
/v2
/v1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ModifiableContextErr"></a>
## type [ModifiableContextErr](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L38-L45>)

ModifiableContextErr is an error that can modify its context.

```go
type ModifiableContextErr interface {
    SetContext(string, map[string]any) ModifiableContextErr
    SetContexts(Context) ModifiableContextErr
    SetTag(string, string) ModifiableContextErr
    SetTags(Tags) ModifiableContextErr
    Unshare() ModifiableContextErr
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="Errorf"></a>
### func [Errorf](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L90>)

```go
func Errorf(format string, args ...any) ModifiableContextErr
```

Errorf creates a new context\-aware error [Err](<#Err>) with a formatted message.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
)

func main() {
	err := ctxerror.Errorf("open %s", "report.csv").
		SetTag("op", "open").
		SetContext("file", map[string]any{"path": "report.csv"})

	fmt.Println(err.Error())
	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetTags(err)["op"])
	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetContext(err)["file"]["path"])

}
```

#### Output

```
open report.csv
open
report.csv
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L81>)

```go
func New(msg string) ModifiableContextErr
```

New creates a new context\-aware error [Err](<#Err>) with the provided message and stack trace. Shared metadata is the default.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
)

func main() {
	err := ctxerror.New("open file").
		SetTag("op", "open").
		SetContext("file", map[string]any{"path": "report.csv"})

	fmt.Println(err.Error())
	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetTags(err)["op"])
	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetContext(err)["file"]["path"])

}
```

#### Output

```
open file
open
report.csv
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Wrap"></a>
### func [Wrap](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L96>)

```go
func Wrap(err error, msg string) ModifiableContextErr
```

Wrap wraps the given error by creating new context\-aware error [Err](<#Err>) with the provided message and stack trace. Shared metadata is the default.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
)

func main() {
	err := ctxerror.Wrap(errors.New("permission denied"), "open file").
		SetTag("op", "open").
		SetContext("file", map[string]any{"path": "report.csv"})
	fmt.Println(err.Error())
	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetTags(err)["op"])
	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetContext(err)["file"]["path"])

}
```

#### Output

```
open file: permission denied
open
report.csv
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Wrapf"></a>
### func [Wrapf](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L105>)

```go
func Wrapf(err error, format string, args ...any) ModifiableContextErr
```

Wrapf wraps the given error by creating a new context\-aware error [Err](<#Err>) with a formatted message and stack trace. Shared metadata is the default.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
)

func main() {
	err := ctxerror.Wrapf(errors.New("permission denied"), "open %s", "report.csv").
		SetTag("op", "open").
		SetContext("file", map[string]any{"path": "report.csv"})
	fmt.Println(err.Error())
	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetTags(err)["op"])
	fmt.Println(ctxerror.GetContext(err)["file"]["path"])

}
```

#### Output

```
open report.csv: permission denied
open
report.csv
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Tags"></a>
## type [Tags](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L35>)

Tags is a small set of string key/value attributes attached to an error chain. Use Tags for lightweight attributes with low cardinality \(for example \`operation\` or \`region\`\). Tags are intended for structured logging and error catchers \(e.g. Sentry\).

```go
type Tags = map[string]string
```

<a name="GetTags"></a>
### func [GetTags](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/ctxerror/context_error.go#L406>)

```go
func GetTags(err error) Tags
```

GetTags returns the tags of the given error chain. If there are multiple tags maps in the chain, they will be merged together. If there are duplicate keys, the value of the outermost error will be used. If no tags were set, an empty tags map is returned. Do not modify the returned tags directly.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/ctxerror"
)

func main() {
	base := ctxerror.New("db error").
		SetTag("env", "prod")
	wrapped := ctxerror.Wrap(base, "service error").
		SetTag("region", "eu")

	tags := ctxerror.GetTags(wrapped)
	fmt.Println(tags["env"], tags["region"])

}
```

#### Output

```
prod eu
```

</p>
</details>
